/blog/agents-need-receipts/.contentHtml:
BLOG

Agents need receipts

Agent-assisted work, project files, review, and technical memory.

Home / Blog / Agents need receipts

NOTE

Project memory

I use agents for software work, writing, review, and research, but I do not treat a chat session as a project memory.

If an agent changes a repository, the relevant material has to move back into the repository: files, notes, assumptions, validation, and review. Otherwise the work stays trapped in a transcript that is difficult to reopen and easy to misread.

This is the part of agentic work I care about most. Not autonomy as a spectacle, not demos pretending to be systems, not chains of tools running without context. I care about the layer where work becomes inspectable: source files, state, reports, pull requests, tests, and human decisions.

The chat can help think. It can compare options, draft alternatives, find inconsistencies, and expose weak assumptions. That is useful. But the project is still the repository, the documents, the code, the checks, and the decisions that remain after the chat is closed.

The more powerful the assistance becomes, the more important this layer gets. Generated work without a trace is not faster engineering. It is future confusion written at high speed.

LINKS

Related routes

AgenticCareerBoostRepository workflow behind this site.RepositoryFiles, reports, state, and public pages.

/blog/sprint-review-agenticcareerboost/.contentHtml:
BLOG

Sprint review

Cleaning project state, public routes, site copy, and repository alignment.

Home / Blog / Sprint review

NOTE

Project state

This sprint was mostly cleanup: root URL, public copy, duplicated state, stale reports, and pages that had started to describe the project in different ways.

That kind of drift is easy to miss. The site still loads, the README still looks organized, and the state files still have dates. But after enough changes, each surface starts carrying a slightly different version of the project.

The public site now lives at the GitHub Pages root. AgenticCareerBoost remains the source project behind it: workflow files, reports, state documents, site source, validation scripts, and public artifacts.

The copy still needed correction. Some pages had become too careful, too symmetrical, and too self-explanatory. That is the wrong direction. The site should state the work, link the files, and leave the reader enough room to judge.

The next cleanup should remove the remaining defensive style across the whole public surface.

LINKS

Related routes

AgenticCareerBoostThe project page for the workflow behind the site.RepositoryThe files behind the project, reports, and site.

/blog/static-sites-as-workbenches/.contentHtml:
BLOG

Static sites as workbenches

Static publishing, route structures, and plain files for personal technical work.

Home / Blog / Static sites as workbenches

NOTE

Static publishing

I like static sites for personal technical work because the structure stays visible. HTML, CSS, a little JavaScript, JSON indexes, assets, and routes.

That does not make static sites inherently better. It just fits this case. A small technical site with projects, notes, CV, and contact routes does not need a larger stack before the content itself has grown enough to justify it.

The useful part is that edits stay close to the files. A route changes. A paragraph changes. A JSON entry changes. The repository keeps the history, and the page remains simple enough to inspect without starting another project around the project.

This also makes writing harder to hide. If the copy is weak, the site has nowhere to bury it. If a project page is vague, the structure will not rescue it.

The current site should stay small until the work itself demands more structure.

LINKS

Related routes

HomeThe current public surface.Site sourceThe static files behind the page.

Profile

Software engineer in Barcelona finishing Computer Engineering at UOC, focused on ML/data systems. I bring a previous banking and insurance operations background, which helps me care about reliability, traceability, and domain rules.

Software engineer building small tools, static pages, reports, and review habits around my own work. I use agents, but I keep the final shape under human judgment.

Software engineer with Java/Spring Boot backend work, document/tooling depth, and 15 years of banking and insurance operations before engineering.

/hire/agentic/.contentHtml:
FOCUS / AGENTIC

Agentic systems

Scoped automation, file-based state, review gates, technical documentation, and developer tooling.

Home / Focus views / Agentic

START

Agentic systems

My work with agentic systems focuses on orchestration: context boundaries, file-based state, review gates, logs, tests, and human approval where outputs affect public or repository surfaces.

AgenticCareerBoost is the main artifact for this view. It turns those rules into source files, public pages, reports, state updates, validation checks, and pull requests.

Agentic CV viewTooling, documentation, static publishing, and reviewable outputs.AgenticCareerBoostThe main project behind this angle.Agents need receiptsA note on agentic work and project memory.

/hire/backend/.contentHtml:
FOCUS / BACKEND

Backend and tooling

Java/Spring base, domain rules, technical documentation, and platform-oriented work.

Home / Focus views / Backend

START

Backend and tooling

This view is for backend/platform conversations about behavior, boundaries, tooling, and maintainability. Java/Spring is part of the route, not the whole identity.

I am most interested in backend work when the domain has real shape: rules, transitions, permissions, failures, and enough complexity to reward clear structure.

IronBank shows the older backend thread. P3CTeX shows tooling and documentation depth. AgenticCareerBoost sits above both as a public system where source, state, reports, and checks have to stay aligned.

Backend CV viewJava/Spring, older backend work, and tooling base.IronBankOlder banking simulation with Java/Spring structure.P3CTeXTooling and document engineering depth.

/projects/agentic-career-boost/.contentHtml:
PROJECTS / AGENTICCAREERBOOST

AgenticCareerBoost

Workflow files, reports, state documents, validation scripts, and public pages.

Home / Projects / AgenticCareerBoost

01A / PROJECT

Project

AgenticCareerBoost is the repository behind this site. It contains role files, workflow instructions, state documents, reports, site source, validation scripts, and pull request history.

Stable rules live in `docs/core`. Current state lives in `state`. Public pages live in `site`. Reports live in `content/reports`.

The public route is deployment-derived at runtime. The repository keeps the source material behind the site, CV, reports, notes, and workflow.

PROJECTS / P3CTEX

P3CTeX

LaTeX tooling for reports, diagrams, listings, CV material, and repeatable document builds.

Home / Projects / P3CTeX

01B / PROJECT

Project

P3CTeX is my LaTeX class and package work for academic and technical documents.

It handles page structure, localized academic labels, code listings, screenshots, diagrams, covers, headers, report conventions, and CV/report builds.

The related p3cTeX-UMLST work extracts UML and listing behavior into a smaller package.
